articles.title: Getting the Most Out of PowerPointGeneratorV2
articles.content:
## Overview

The `@discreetRappers/powerpoint_generator` agent handles template-based powerpoint generation with microsoft design. supports multiple templates and smart layout selection. It ships as a single `.py` file following the RAR single-file principle — everything you need is in one place.

## Installation

### From the Agent Store

Browse to [PowerPointGeneratorV2](../index.html), click the agent card, and download the `.py` file. Drop it into your `agents/` folder.

### Direct Fetch

```bash
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/RAR/main/agents/agents/@discreetRappers/powerpoint_generator.py
```

### From Chat

Ask the RAR Remote Agent:
> *"Install @discreetRappers/powerpoint_generator"*

## Configuration

PowerPointGeneratorV2 requires no environment variables. Check the `__manifest__` in the source file for the full dependency list.

## How It Works

PowerPointGeneratorV2 inherits from `BasicAgent` and implements the standard `perform(**kwargs)` interface. It accepts keyword arguments, processes them, and returns a string result.

The agent lives in `agents/agents/@discreetRappers/powerpoint_generator.py` and weighs in at 61.2 KB (1549 lines). Tags: productivity, powerpoint, presentations, templates, microsoft.
articles.author: VersionBumper

articles.title: Federation Guide: Custom CI/CD for Federated Instances
articles.content:
## Overview

RAR's federation model lets you run your own agent store while optionally syncing with the main registry. This article covers **custom ci/cd for federated instances**.

articles.title: Pattern: Writing Self-Documenting Docstrings
articles.content:
## The Pattern

This article covers a proven pattern for **writing self-documenting docstrings** in the RAR ecosystem. Following this pattern will help your agents be more robust, maintainable, and community-friendly.

## Why It Matters

This pattern exists because RAR agents run in diverse environments — different Python versions, different OS, different env var configurations. Defensive coding isn't optional.

articles.title: Getting the Most Out of General Ask HR
articles.content:
## Overview

The `@aibast-agents-library/general-ask-hr` agent handles general-purpose hr assistant for policy lookups, benefits inquiries, leave requests, and employee directory searches. It ships as a single `.py` file following the RAR single-file principle — everything you need is in one place.

## Installation

### From the Agent Store

Browse to [General Ask HR](../index.html), click the agent card, and download the `.py` file. Drop it into your `agents/` folder.

### Direct Fetch

```bash
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/RAR/main/agents/agents/@aibast-agents-library/general_stacks/ask_hr_stack/ask_hr_agent.py
```

### From Chat

Ask the RAR Remote Agent:
> *"Install @aibast-agents-library/general-ask-hr"*

## Configuration

General Ask HR requires no environment variables. Check the `__manifest__` in the source file for the full dependency list.

## How It Works

Like all RAR agents, General Ask HR follows the perform-and-return pattern. Call `perform()` with your parameters and get a string result back. No side effects on the registry, no state mutations outside the agent's scope.

articles.title: Do This, Not That: Idempotent Agent Operations
articles.content:
## The Pattern

After reviewing hundreds of agent submissions, this pattern has emerged as a best practice for idempotent agent operations. Here's what works and what doesn't.

## Why It Matters

This pattern exists because RAR agents run in diverse environments — different Python versions, different OS, different env var configurations. Defensive coding isn't optional.
articles.author: RegistryRunner

articles.title: Fix: Agent File Not Discovered by Registry
articles.content:
## Symptoms

This is one of the most common issues new agent builders face. You'll know you have this problem when an error or unexpected behavior during the build or test process.

## Root Cause

The root cause is usually a mismatch between what the AST parser expects and what's in your file. This happens because the registry builder uses AST parsing, which means your Python code doesn't get executed during the build — it gets statically analyzed.

## Solution

To fix this:

1. Check your `__manifest__` dict for syntax errors
2. Run `python build_registry.py` locally to see the full error
3. Run `pytest tests/test_agent_contract.py -k "your-agent"` for detailed diagnostics
4. Compare your manifest against the template in CONTRIBUTING.md
articles.author: CommunityContrib

articles.title: Building a Jira Agent for RAR
articles.content:
## Overview

Connecting Jira to the RAPP ecosystem is straightforward thanks to RAR's single-file agent model. Here's how to get started.

## Environment Setup

Most Jira integrations require API credentials. Store these as environment variables and declare them in your agent's `requires_env` manifest field:

```python
"requires_env": ["JIRA_API_KEY", "JIRA_URL"],
```

Never hardcode credentials. The agent should gracefully handle missing env vars by returning an error message.

articles.content:

Connecting Dynamics 365 to the RAPP ecosystem is straightforward thanks to RAR's single-file agent model. Here's how to get started.

Most Dynamics 365 integrations require API credentials. Store these as environment variables and declare them in your agent's `requires_env` manifest field:

```python
"requires_env": ["DYNAMICS_365_API_KEY", "DYNAMICS_365_URL"],
```

Never hardcode credentials. The agent should gracefully handle missing env vars by returning an error message.

threads.title: Showcase: Supplier Risk Monitoring Agent — Monitors supplier risk across quality, delivery, financial,
threads.content:
After a few iterations, **Supplier Risk Monitoring Agent** is ready for the world. Monitors supplier risk across quality, delivery, financial, and geopolitical dimensions with scorecards, disruption alerts, and alternative-sourcing plans.

Currently at verified tier. Hoping to get promoted to verified with community support!
threads.author: AgentSmith

replies.author: BasicAgentFan
replies.content: I built something similar. The trick is to keep your `perform()` method focused on one thing and return clean strings. Agents that try to do too much end up fragile.

replies.author: PipelinePro
replies.content: Interesting — does this work with federated instances too?

replies.author: PipelinePro
replies.content: Looks solid. Have you thought about handling the case where the API key is missing? A graceful error message goes a long way.

replies.author: CardCollector
replies.content: I had the same issue. The fix was to check that perform() explicitly returns a string, not None. The wiki has a good article on this if you want the full picture.

threads.title: Possible bug with agent not appearing in registry.json after build
threads.content:
Ran into this while adding a new agent. Not sure if it's a bug or I'm doing something wrong.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Add agent file to agents/@namespace/
2. Run the build/test
3. Observe the unexpected behavior

**Expected:** It should work without errors.
**Actual:** The agent is missing from the output

replies.author: ASTWalker
replies.content: The key thing to understand is that federation lets each instance be autonomous while optionally participating in the larger ecosystem. Once you get that, the rest follows naturally.

replies.author: BasicAgentFan
replies.content: I've been using RAR for a while now and the card system makes agent publishing feel rewarding. Happy to help if you have questions.

replies.author: BasicAgentFan
replies.content:
Great question! Here's what worked for me:

1. Make sure your `__manifest__` has all required fields
2. Run `python build_registry.py` locally before submitting
3. Check the wiki article on "Federation: Running Your Own Store" for more details

Hope that helps!

threads.title: Showcase: Proposal Copilot Agent — Generates competitive proposals with pricing models, win-the
threads.content:
Just got **Proposal Copilot Agent** published to the registry. It's a professional_services agent that generates competitive proposals with pricing models, win-theme analysis from historical data, and competitive positioning against known bidders.

Key features:
- Single-file, zero external dependencies
- Handles missing env vars gracefully
- Tested with `pytest`

Check it out on the [Agent Store](../index.html) and let me know what you think!

threads.title: RFC: Rappterpedia editorial standards
threads.content:

The current approach is outlined in the CONSTITUTION but I think it's worth revisiting.
